decisions.title_en: EU Council & Parliament agree on AI Act omnibus — high-risk delay + deepfake ban
decisions.reason: Provisional political deal struck overnight 5-7 to delay high-risk AI Act enforcement to Dec 2027 (16-month delay), expand SME exemptions to small mid-caps (≤500 employees), shorten transparency-watermark grace period to 3 months, and add EU-wide ban on non-consensual deepfakes / CSAM. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI ships GPT-Realtime-2 + Translate + Whisper voice API
decisions.reason: OpenAI unveiled three new realtime voice models on 5-7: GPT-Realtime-2 (first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning), GPT-Realtime-Translate (live speech translation 70+→13 languages), GPT-Realtime-Whisper (streaming STT). API also gains MCP server, image input, SIP phone calling. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: Hassett: WH preparing FDA-style mandatory AI vetting EO within 2 weeks
decisions.reason: Previously published 5-4 (EO drafting) and 5-5 (CAISI signs voluntary agreements with Google/Microsoft/xAI). NEW info on 5-6: NEC Director Kevin Hassett tells Bloomberg the order being studied would vet new AI models 'like the FDA evaluates drugs' — escalating from voluntary CAISI process to potentially MANDATORY pre-release testing. Hassett expects EO signed within two weeks. This is a substantive new development that re-shapes the policy trajectory.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic Code w/ Claude SF — Dreaming, Code Review, Remote Agents, 17x API growth
decisions.reason: Anthropic developer conference 5-6 in San Francisco. Key launches: 'Dreaming' memory consolidation feature for Claude managed agents (runs between sessions to merge/de-dupe/refresh memory), expanded Code Review + CI Auto-Fix, Remote Agents (control laptop from phone), doubled Claude Code 5h limit for Pro/Max/Enterprise. Reveal: API volume up 17x YoY. story_key not seen in lookback (Anthropic-SpaceX deal is different angle).

decisions.title_en: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite hits GA at $0.25/M input, Elo 1432
decisions.reason: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite moved from preview (March 3) to general availability on 5-7 via Gemini API + Vertex AI. Pricing $0.25/M input + $1.50/M output, 2.5x faster TTFT, 45% faster output, Elo 1432 on Arena.ai, 86.9% GPQA Diamond. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: Bloomberg — Anthropic shifting Claude toward consumer use cases
decisions.reason: Bloomberg report 5-7: Anthropic, traditionally B2B-focused, has been working since late 2025 to make Claude more consumer-friendly — improving health/travel/recipe answers, dropping mobile app cold-start from 5-6s to ~1s. Claude is currently #2 free app on US App Store. story_key not seen in lookback (different angle from finance JV / SpaceX deal).

decisions.title_en: DeepMind UK staff vote 98% to unionize over Pentagon + IDF use
decisions.reason: 98% of DeepMind UK CWU members voted to form what would be world's first union at a frontier AI lab. Workers letter to management on ~5-5 demands recognition over Pentagon Gemini deal (DoD 'any lawful purpose') and Israeli military use. Affects ~1,000 London staff. 10 working days for management to recognize voluntarily. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: Jensen Huang — AI doom CEOs have 'god complex'; Anthropic fires back
decisions.reason: Nvidia's Jensen Huang publicly criticized Dario Amodei's 50% entry-level white-collar job loss prediction as 'ridiculous' and accused fellow CEOs of having a 'god complex'. Anthropic spokesperson responded 5-5 saying Dario has never claimed 'only Anthropic' can build safe AI. Yann LeCun separately called doom rhetoric 'extremely destructive'. story_key not seen in lookback (amodei-mythos-moment-of-danger from 5-5 was about cyber risks, not jobs/doom debate).

decisions.title_en: Blitzy raises $200M at $1.4B valuation for autonomous coding
decisions.reason: Cambridge MA's Blitzy raised $200M at $1.4B valuation 5-5, led by Northzone with PSG/Battery/Jump/Morgan Creek joining. Founded 2023 by ex-NVIDIA architect Sid Pardeshi + Brian Elliott. Reverse-engineers enterprise codebases >100M LOC, runs thousands of agents in parallel. Boston's newest unicorn. Adopted by State Street, QAD. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: CoreWeave Q1 2026 — revenue >100% YoY to ~$1.97B
decisions.reason: CoreWeave reported Q1 2026 results 5-7. Revenue jumped over 100% YoY to ~$1.97B; options market priced 18.7% post-earnings move. Continues string of AI infra companies clearing high revenue bars. story_key not seen in lookback (separate from coreweave-anthropic-cloud-deal type stories).

decisions.title_en: OpenAI gives 8000 GPT-5.5 party applicants 10x Codex rate limit
decisions.reason: After OpenAI's invite-only 'GPT-5.5 on 5/5' party drew 8000+ applicants for limited spots, OpenAI emailed all applicants — accepted, waitlisted, or rejected — a 10x Codex rate-limit boost on personal ChatGPT accounts effective immediately through June 5. Notable signal of competitive pressure for developer mindshare vs. Anthropic. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI rolls out CPC bidding + self-serve Ads Manager beta for ChatGPT
decisions.reason: OpenAI announced 5-7 expanded ChatGPT ads with new partner-buy and beta self-serve Ads Manager, introducing CPC bidding + expanded measurement tools. Significant step in monetization beyond Plus/Enterprise subs. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: Microsoft Q1 2026 AI diffusion report — UAE 70.1%, US 21st, 26 economies >30%
decisions.reason: Microsoft Global AI Diffusion Q1 2026 published 5-7 on On The Issues blog. AI usage rose +1.5pp QoQ to 17.8% of global working-age population. UAE leads 70.1%; US moved 24th→21st (31.3%); 26 economies now >30% adoption. South Korea/Thailand/Japan biggest movers. story_key not seen in lookback.

decisions.title_en: Musk-Altman trial week 2 closes — Zilis testifies Musk offered Altman Tesla board seat
decisions.reason: Returning story heavily covered (lookback shows 4-30, 5-1, 5-4, 5-5). NEW substantive info on 5-6/5-7: Shivon Zilis (mother of Musk's 4 children, ex-OpenAI board) testified in Oakland court she discussed structure with Altman/Brockman in 2017-18, said Musk offered Altman a Tesla board seat — fresh angle on the personal/governance entanglement. Brockman finished testimony 5-5. Trial wrapping its second week.
